tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests import and call handle_hf_error, execute subprocess simulations of the closure pattern, parse ASTs to verify structural properties, and check actual runtime behavior (recursion, exception handling). They are not purely text-matching tests.

test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests verify real behavior: test_closure_no_self_recursion runs a subprocess that would hit recursion limit with the buggy code; test_stop_iteration_handled verifies StopIteration is caught and converted to Error (a stub would leak StopIteration); test_empty_exception_non_empty_message verifies non-empty messages for bare exceptions (a stub raising Error('') would fail).

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution/solve.sh file exists in the container and contains the exact gold patch. An agent could read /repo/../solution/solve.sh or the solution directory to obtain the exact fix. There's no evidence the solution directory is excluded from the agent's filesystem access.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors specified in instruction.md are tested: kwargs.pop('fn') pattern (test_closure_no_self_recursion), StopIteration handling (test_stop_iteration_handled), non-empty error messages (test_empty_exception_non_empty_message), 401/auth errors (test_401_error_still_handled), 429/TooManyRequests (test_too_many_requests_still_handled), if-branches and raise counts (test_handle_hf_error_not_stub).
